# Governance Catalog Access

Manage catalog, governance, access-control, retention, classification, audit, and compliance workflows.

### kiri attr detect-pii

Ask Kiri to classify PII on one column...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri attr detect-pii [OPTIONS] REF
```

### Arguments

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `REF` | <asset_urn>#<column_name> | yes |  |  |

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--apply` | Apply the suggestion to the column (flips is_pii/pii_category/classification/masking_policy). Defaults to suggest-only - human review is the norm per. | no | False |  |
| `--actor` | Override actor identity (defaults to env). | no |  |  |

### kiri attr list

List catalog columns with their review...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri attr list [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--state` | Filter by review_state: new \| in_progress \| submitted \| approved \| rejected. Omit to show all. | no |  |  |
| `--domain` | Filter by the owning asset's domain. | no |  |  |
| `--source` | Filter by the column's discovery_source URN substring. Useful for triaging 'what did the last Salesforce sync discover?' lists. | no |  |  |
| `--limit` | Maximum rows to print. | no | 50 |  |

## kiri audit

Audit-log operations.

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri audit [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

None.

### Subcommands

- [`kiri audit correlate`](#kiri-audit-correlate) - Run a post-hoc audit correlation against...
- [`kiri audit join`](#kiri-audit-join) - Join Kirimana audit + Databricks audit...
- [`kiri audit purge`](#kiri-audit-purge) - *** Use ``kiri audit redact`` instead.
- [`kiri audit redact`](#kiri-audit-redact) - Redact a log row in place + append a...

### kiri audit correlate

Run a post-hoc audit correlation against...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri audit correlate [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--since` | Start of the audit window (ISO-8601). Inclusive. E.g. '2026-05-12T00:00:00+00:00'. | yes |  |  |
| `--until` | End of the audit window (ISO-8601). Exclusive - next day's batch picks up the boundary event. | yes |  |  |
| `--gate-log` | Path to the JSONL gate-audit log (one GateAuditEntry model-dump per line). Missing or empty file is treated as zero gate events - every Databricks AI query in the window becomes a BYPASS finding. Mutually exclusive with --gate-store. | no |  |  |
| `--gate-store` | Connection string for a persistent gate-audit store (AI-roadmap #6a). Format: 'jsonl:<path>' for the file backend, or 'postgresql+psycopg://user:pass@host/db' for the Postgres backend (requires the kiri-core [postgres-store] extra). Mutually exclusive with --gate-log. | no |  |  |
| `--max-gate-age` | Seconds: a gate decision older than this triggers STALE_GATE. Default 3600 (1 hour) - reflects the 'one Genie session' norm. | no | 3600 |  |
| `--join-window` | Seconds: when matching by user (no trace-id propagated), the gate decision must be within this many seconds before the Databricks query. Default 60. | no | 60 |  |
| `--ai-only`, `--all-traffic` | Filter to AI-driven traffic (trace-id present or Genie tag). Default true. Use --all-traffic to audit every SQL execution in the window - expect many BYPASS false-positives from human users. | no | True |  |
| `--output` | Output format: 'rich' (default) or 'json'. | no | rich |  |
| `--dispatch`, `--no-dispatch` | Slice 2d wiring - when --dispatch is on AND the project has an ``incidents:`` block configured, each finding above --severity-floor is routed to the configured ITSM dispatcher. Default off so the CLI stays non-fatal for ad-hoc operator runs. | no | False |  |
| `--severity-floor` | When --dispatch is on, only findings whose mapped severity is at-or-above this floor are dispatched. Mapping: BYPASS / FORBIDDEN_QUERY_RAN â†’ critical, STALE_GATE â†’ medium. Default 'low' (every finding dispatched). Tighten to 'critical' to alert only on gate-bypass + forbidden-query events. | no | low |  |
| `--project` | Project root. | no |  |  |

### kiri audit join

Join Kirimana audit + Databricks audit...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri audit join [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--apply-id` | Filter events to one apply run. The Kirimana side carries this on the apply-log row; the Databricks side carries it via the trace comment / Workflow tag map. Mutually exclusive with --trace-id. | no |  |  |
| `--trace-id` | Filter events to one trace-id. Useful when an apply fans into multiple sub-callers and you want to follow the root trace specifically. | no |  |  |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project root. Sources resolve relative to here. | no |  |  |
| `--audit-log` | AI Gateway audit JSONL. Defaults to ``<project>/logs/audit.jsonl``. | no |  |  |
| `--incidents-log` | Incidents JSONL. Defaults to ``<project>/logs/incidents.jsonl``. | no |  |  |
| `--databricks-audit-dir` | Directory holding Databricks-delivered audit JSON / JSONL files. Production deploys rsync from the ADLS audit-logs container into ``<project>/logs/databricks-audit/``. | no |  |  |
| `--since` | Time window - only events newer than ``now - since``. Format: ``<int>{d\|h\|m}``, e.g. ``1d``, ``6h``, ``30m``. | no |  |  |
| `--format` | Output format: text (default, human-readable) \| json (machine). | no | text |  |

### kiri audit purge

*** Use ``kiri audit redact`` instead.

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri audit purge [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--trace-id` | (unused; see error) | no |  |  |

### Examples

```bash
kiri audit purge`` is intentionally not implemented. Hard-deleting
```

### kiri audit redact

Redact a log row in place + append a...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri audit redact [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--trace-id` | The id of the row to redact. For audit / MCP logs this is ``trace_id``; for ``--incidents-log`` it is the incident's ``event_id``. | yes |  |  |
| `--reason` | Closed-enum reason (gdpr-art-17 \| data-residency \| platform-rotation \| legal-hold-release \| operator-error). | yes |  | `gdpr-art-17`, `data-residency`, `platform-rotation`, `legal-hold-release`, `operator-error` |
| `--justification` | Free-form text explaining the redaction. Auditors read this - be specific (e.g. 'data subject request DR-2026-042'). | yes |  |  |
| `--audit-log` | Path to the audit log file. Defaults to ./logs/audit.jsonl (the AI Gateway audit log). | no |  |  |
| `--mcp-log` | Redact the MCP audit log (./logs/mcp_audit.jsonl) instead of the AI Gateway one. Mutually exclusive with --audit-log / --incidents-log. | no | False |  |
| `--incidents-log` | Redact the incident dispatch log (./logs/incidents.jsonl) - surface. Mutually exclusive with --audit-log / --mcp-log. Trace_id is interpreted as the incident's event_id. | no | False |  |

### kiri catalog import

Populate the catalog from project YAML, or...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri catalog import [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project`, `-p` | kiri project directory | no |  |  |
| `--db` |  | no |  |  |
| `--from-ndjson` | Bypass the project walk and load Asset records from an NDJSON stream (one JSON Asset per line). Use ``-`` to read stdin - designed to chain with ``kiri catalog pull``. | no |  |  |


None.

#### kiri catalog policy upsert

Insert or update a policy.

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri catalog policy upsert [OPTIONS] POLICY_ID
```

### Arguments

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `POLICY_ID` | Policy id (also the principal) | yes |  |  |

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--name` |  | yes |  |  |
| `--max-class` |  | no | internal | `public`, `internal`, `confidential`, `restricted` |
| `--allow-special-pii`, `--no-special-pii` |  | no | False |  |
| `--hide` | PII category to hide (repeatable) | no |  | `name`, `email`, `phone`, `address`, `government_id`, `auth`, `location`, `payment`, `other`, `health`, `biometric`, `genetic`, `criminal_record`, `political_opinion`, `religious_belief`, `trade_union`, `sexual_orientation` |
| `--domain` | Whitelisted domain (repeatable). Omit for full-domain access; pass --domain '' to lock down to zero domains. | no |  |  |
| `--description` |  | no |  |  |
| `--project`, `-p` |  | no |  |  |
| `--db` |  | no |  |  |

### kiri catalog pull

Pull catalog metadata from an external...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri catalog pull [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--target`, `-t` | Source target: unity | yes |  |  |
| `--catalog` | External catalog name to pull from (Unity catalog). | yes |  |  |
| `--project`, `-p` |  | no |  |  |
| `--schema` | Schema to pull (repeatable). Default: all schemas in the catalog except information_schema. | no |  |  |
| `--no-columns` | Skip column-level metadata (faster inventory pull). | no | False |  |
| `--no-tags` | Skip table-level tag round-trip from properties. | no | False |  |
| `--output`, `-o` | NDJSON output path. Default stdout - one Asset per line. | no |  |  |

### kiri catalog push

Push catalog rows to an external catalog...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri catalog push [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--target`, `-t` | Sink target: unity \| horizon \| purview | yes |  |  |
| `--project`, `-p` |  | no |  |  |
| `--db` |  | no |  |  |
| `--dry-run`, `--no-dry-run` | Default true. --no-dry-run executes the annotation writes against the live catalog via the Unity Catalog executor; inspect the dry-run NDJSON first. | no | True |  |
| `--output`, `-o` | NDJSON output path. Default stdout - one JSON write per line, pipe to jq for inspection. | no |  |  |
| `--type` | Filter by asset type (repeatable). Default all. | no |  | `source`, `bronze`, `silver`, `gold`, `view`, `dashboard`, `job` |
| `--urn` | Filter to specific asset URNs (repeatable). Default every asset in the catalog. | no |  |  |
| `--no-lineage` | Skip lineage edges. | no | False |  |

### kiri catalog verify

Diff live catalog annotations against the...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri catalog verify [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--target` | Named profile from kiri.yml (resolves the platform adapter); defaults to default_target. NB: a deployment target, not a sink kind. | no |  |  |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project root (defaults to cwd). | no |  |  |
| `--db` | Catalog SQLite path (defaults to .kiri/catalog.sqlite). | no |  |  |
| `--fail-on` | Closed menu: 'missing', 'stale', 'extra', or 'all'. Findings of this kind cause exit code 1. | no | all |  |
| `--output`, `-o` | Write the JSON report here (default stdout). | no |  |  |
| `--type` | Restrict to these asset types (repeatable). | no |  | `source`, `bronze`, `silver`, `gold`, `view`, `dashboard`, `job` |
| `--urn` | Restrict to these asset URNs (repeatable). | no |  |  |

## kiri compliance

Compliance reporting.

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri compliance [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

None.

### Subcommands

- [`kiri compliance report`](#kiri-compliance-report) - Generate a compliance report for the...

### kiri compliance report

Generate a compliance report for the...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri compliance report [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--framework`, `-f` | Compliance framework to report on. | yes |  | `dora`, `ai_act`, `gdpr` |
| `--period`, `-p` | Time window ``YYYY-MM-DD..YYYY-MM-DD`` (inclusive). Defaults to the last 365 days. | no |  |  |
| `--format` | Output format: json (default, for GRC import) or markdown. | no | json |  |
| `--project` | Project root. | no |  |  |
| `--fail-on-fail` | Exit non-zero when any control is ``fail`` - useful in CI to gate merges on compliance regressions. | no | False |  |

### Subcommands

- [`kiri governance approval-count`](#kiri-governance-approval-count) - Print the required approver count for a...
- [`kiri governance sync`](#kiri-governance-sync) - Synthesise governance policies from...

### kiri governance approval-count

Print the required approver count for a...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri governance approval-count [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--action` | Action name from kiri_core.approval_policy.ApprovalAction, e.g. ``audit_log_redaction`` / ``catalog_push_live`` / ``contract_retirement``. Use ``kiri governance approval-count --list`` to see all. | no |  |  |
| `--project` | Project root containing kiri.yml. | no |  |  |
| `--list` | Print every supported action name (one per line) and exit. | no | False |  |

### kiri governance sync

Synthesise governance policies from...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri governance sync [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--backend` | Governance backend. Only ``ranger`` supported in MVP. | yes |  |  |
| `--project` | Project root. | no |  |  |
| `--output`, `-o` | Directory to write one JSON file per policy. Defaults to stdout (for piping into curl / jq / Ranger REST). | no |  |  |
| `--service` | Ranger service name to attach policies to. Must match the Trino service configured in Ranger admin. | no | trino_kirimana |  |

## kiri rbac

RBAC administration + CI-time gates

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri rbac [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

None.

### Subcommands

- [`kiri rbac emit-grants`](#kiri-rbac-emit-grants) - Emit silver-layer GRANT statements per...
- [`kiri rbac gate-pr`](#kiri-rbac-gate-pr) - Gate a PR against's authorship...

### kiri rbac emit-grants

Emit silver-layer GRANT statements per...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri rbac emit-grants [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--target`, `-t` | Named profile from kiri.yml | no |  |  |
| `--role` | Role to emit grants for: analyst \| data_engineer \| auditor. | no | analyst |  |
| `--catalog` | Catalog name to qualify the schemas. Default: pulled from the resolved profile (`profile.catalog` or `database`). | no |  |  |
| `--zone` | Zone to emit grants for: raw \| business \| pit \| all (default). | no | all |  |

### kiri rbac gate-pr

Gate a PR against's authorship...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri rbac gate-pr [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--submitter` | PR submitter's stable identity (GitHub login or OIDC sub). | yes |  |  |
| `--groups` | Comma-separated groups the submitter belongs to (e.g. ``acme/hr,acme/compliance``). Falls back to ``KIRIMANA_PR_GATE_GROUPS`` env var. | no |  |  |
| `--changed-files-from` | Path to a newline-delimited file listing changed paths. Defaults to stdin. | no |  |  |
| `--restricted-raises-from` | Optional path to a newline-delimited file listing the changed paths whose diff raised classification to ``restricted``. Workflow computes this via ``git diff`` + grep; leave empty if no such raises are in the PR. | no |  |  |
| `--project-dir` | Project root containing ``kiri_rbac.yml``. | no |  |  |

## kiri retention

Adapter-dispatched data retention

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri retention [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory. Defaults (in order) to KIRIMANA_PROJECT, the nearest kiri.yml above the cwd, then the `kiri use` active project. | no |  |  |
| `--target`, `-t` | Named profile to use (dev\|test\|prod). Falls back to KIRIMANA_TARGET. | no |  |  |
| `--auto` | Dispatch retention for every contract in the project that declares kiri.retention.days. Required - there is no per-table invocation; retention is a project-level sweep. | no | False |  |
| `--layer` | Restrict to one medallion layer (bronze\|silver\|gold). Defaults to all layers. | no |  |  |
| `--dry-run` | Preview what each adapter would do - no statements are executed. The selection logic + per-table command list is identical to --auto. | no | False |  |
